generation.note: Reference memos produced with the review agent's model against the committed designs; flaw difficulty is bounded by construction. Regenerate live with your own key: python eval/harness.py --live

clean_00.recommendation: Approve as designed: signup flow simplification is properly powered, monitored on a pre-committed boundary, and free of exposure or multiplicity risks.
clean_00.analysis_plan: Two-proportion z-test on the primary rate; add CUPED with a 28-day pre-period covariate to cut variance. Keep the scheduled-look alpha-spending plan for monitoring.

clean_01.recommendation: Approve as designed: upload pipeline speedup is properly powered, monitored on a pre-committed boundary, and free of exposure or multiplicity risks.
clean_01.analysis_plan: Welch t-test on the primary metric; add CUPED with a 28-day pre-period covariate to cut variance. Keep the scheduled-look alpha-spending plan for monitoring.

clean_02.recommendation: Approve as designed: creator payout change is properly powered, monitored on a pre-committed boundary, and free of exposure or multiplicity risks.
clean_02.analysis_plan: Welch t-test on the mean with a Mann-Whitney U robustness check, since the metric is zero-inflated with a heavy right tail; add CUPED with a 28-day pre-period covariate to cut variance. Keep the scheduled-look alpha-spending plan for monitoring.

clean_03.analysis_plan: Welch t-test on the primary metric; add CUPED with a 28-day pre-period covariate to cut variance. Keep the scheduled-look alpha-spending plan for monitoring.

single_00.recommendation: Revise before launch: fix peeking, then this is a decision-grade experiment; the traffic and metric choices are otherwise sound.

flags.rationale: The monitoring plan stops at the first significant daily p-value with no sequential correction, so the stated alpha is fiction.
flags.quantification: Measured in the lab: 14 daily uncorrected looks yield a 24.1% false positive rate against a nominal 5% (4,000 simulated nulls); the O'Brien-Fleming boundary restores 4.95%.
flags.fix: Pre-commit a Lan-DeMets alpha-spending boundary for the daily looks, or restrict to the scheduled interim and final readouts.

single_00.analysis_plan: Two-proportion z-test on the primary rate; add CUPED with a 28-day pre-period covariate to cut variance. Keep the scheduled-look alpha-spending plan for monitoring.

single_01.recommendation: Revise before launch: fix multiple comparisons, then this is a decision-grade experiment; the traffic and metric choices are otherwise sound.

flags.rationale: 20 metrics are tested for significance with correction 'none', so some metric will look significant by chance in most experiments.
flags.quantification: Evidence pack: expected family-wise error rate 0.642 if uncorrected; the lab measures 62.9% for 20 independent null metrics, and Benjamini-Hochberg holds FDR at 4.8%.
flags.fix: Name one primary decision metric; apply Benjamini-Hochberg to the remaining metrics as exploratory.

single_01.analysis_plan: Welch t-test on the primary metric; add CUPED with a 28-day pre-period covariate to cut variance. Keep the scheduled-look alpha-spending plan for monitoring.

single_02.recommendation: Revise before launch: fix contamination, then this is a decision-grade experiment; the traffic and metric choices are otherwise sound.

flags.rationale: Exposure notes describe two-sided non-compliance (shared devices exposing control, logged-out sessions unexposing treatment), and the plan compares users by what they experienced, which is per-protocol selection bias.
flags.quantification: Measured in the lab at 20%/10% contamination with a true effect of 0.50: per-protocol reads 0.85, intent-to-treat attenuates to 0.38, and the IV/Wald estimator recovers 0.54.
flags.fix: Analyze intent-to-treat by assignment for the decision, and report the IV/Wald estimate (ITT divided by the compliance gap) for the per-user effect.

single_02.analysis_plan: Welch t-test on the mean with a Mann-Whitney U robustness check, since the metric is zero-inflated with a heavy right tail; add CUPED with a 28-day pre-period covariate to cut variance. Keep the scheduled-look alpha-spending plan for monitoring.

single_03.recommendation: Revise before launch: fix interference, then this is a decision-grade experiment; the traffic and metric choices are otherwise sound.

flags.rationale: The share-to-friends component moves outcomes through the follow graph while randomization is at the user level, so control users are partially treated and the contrast collapses.
flags.quantification: Measured in the lab: with direct effect 0.5 and spillover 0.3 (global effect 0.8), user-level randomization estimates 0.52, missing the spillover entirely; cluster randomization recovers 0.83.
flags.fix: Randomize connected clusters of the follow graph rather than users, and size the experiment on the effective number of clusters.

single_03.analysis_plan: Welch t-test on the primary metric; add CUPED with a 28-day pre-period covariate to cut variance. Keep the scheduled-look alpha-spending plan for monitoring.

single_04.recommendation: Revise before launch: fix subgroup fishing, then this is a decision-grade experiment; the traffic and metric choices are otherwise sound.

flags.rationale: The plan scans 20 country segments post hoc and reports whatever reaches significance, which manufactures discoveries under the null.
flags.quantification: Measured in the lab: scanning 20 null segments finds at least one 'significant' subgroup in 63.8% of experiments, about one false subgroup per experiment.
flags.fix: Pre-register the segments that matter to the decision and apply Benjamini-Hochberg to any exploratory scan.

single_04.analysis_plan: Two-proportion z-test on the primary rate; add CUPED with a 28-day pre-period covariate to cut variance. Keep the scheduled-look alpha-spending plan for monitoring.

single_05.recommendation: Revise before launch: fix novelty too short, then this is a decision-grade experiment; the traffic and metric choices are otherwise sound.

flags.rationale: A visibly noticeable redesign generates a novelty spike, and a 7-day readout averages that spike into the estimate.
flags.quantification: Measured in the lab with a decaying effect (long-run 0.10, novelty amplitude 0.40, 5-day time constant): the week-1 readout gives 0.318, a 3.2x overstatement of the long-run effect; a post-burn-in window reads 0.13.
flags.fix: Extend to at least four weeks and base the launch decision on a post-burn-in window, or model the decay explicitly.

single_05.analysis_plan: Welch t-test on the primary metric; add CUPED with a 28-day pre-period covariate to cut variance. Keep the scheduled-look alpha-spending plan for monitoring.

single_06.recommendation: Revise before launch: fix underpowered, then this is a decision-grade experiment; the traffic and metric choices are otherwise sound.

flags.rationale: The design cannot detect its own target effect at the stated power target, so a null readout is uninformative and a significant one is exaggerated.
flags.quantification: Evidence pack: achieved power 0.198 against a 0.80 target; 3,920 users per arm versus 25,117 required.
flags.fix: Extend the run or widen the ramp to reach the required sample per arm, or re-scope the minimum detectable effect the team actually needs.

single_06.analysis_plan: Welch t-test on the mean with a Mann-Whitney U robustness check, since the metric is zero-inflated with a heavy right tail; add CUPED with a 28-day pre-period covariate to cut variance. Keep the scheduled-look alpha-spending plan for monitoring.

flags.fix: Pre-commit a Lan-DeMets alpha-spending boundary for the daily looks, or restrict to the scheduled interim and final readouts.
